description: Mobile Workout Button Relocation & Badge Cleanup - Pin Start Workout button and active workout banner to bottom of screen, remove redundant burn buddy/squad badges from list cards

userStories.title: Move Start Workout button to fixed bottom position (Web)
userStories.description: As a mobile web user, I want the Start Workout button pinned above the bottom nav bar so I can always start a workout without scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Start Workout button renders in a fixed-position container above the bottom navigation bar on mobile viewports (below md breakpoint) in apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx
Button is hidden on desktop layout (above md breakpoint) — desktop keeps current inline placement or omits the fixed element entirely
Button remains full-width and uses existing orange/primary styling (bg-primary, text-white, rounded-lg)
The fixed bottom container spans full width with dark theme styling matching the nav bar (bg-black/90, border-t border-gray-800)

Scroll content has enough bottom padding so no content is hidden behind the fixed button — increase the existing pb-20 in apps/web/src/app/(main)/layout.tsx to accommodate both nav bar and workout area
Tapping the button still opens the workout type selector modal via setShowWorkoutSelector(true)
The original inline Start Workout button markup (around line 311-321 in page.tsx) is removed from the scroll content on mobile viewports
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Move active workout banner to fixed bottom position (Web)
userStories.description: As a mobile web user with an active workout, I want the active workout controls pinned at the bottom so I can always access Strike/End actions.
acceptanceCriteria: When an active workout exists, the active workout banner replaces the Start Workout button in the fixed bottom area on mobile viewports
Banner shows the workout type label, elapsed time display, and Strike/End Workout action buttons
Banner is fixed above the bottom nav bar on mobile viewports (below md breakpoint)
On desktop (above md breakpoint), active workout banner stays in its current inline position at the top of page content
The original inline active workout banner markup (around lines 292-309 in page.tsx) is hidden on mobile viewports since it now appears in the fixed bottom area
Strike and End Workout button handlers still function correctly from the new position
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Move Start Workout button to fixed bottom position (Mobile App)
userStories.description: As a React Native mobile user, I want the Start Workout button pinned at the bottom of the screen so I can always start a workout without scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Start Workout button renders outside the ScrollView in apps/mobile/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx, positioned at the bottom of the screen container
Button sits above the safe area bottom inset (use SafeAreaView or appropriate bottom padding)
The original inline Start Workout button markup (around lines 358-367) is removed from inside the ScrollView
ScrollView content is not obscured by the button — add appropriate bottom padding to the ScrollView contentContainerStyle
Tapping the button still opens the workout type selector modal via setShowWorkoutSelector(true)
Button keeps its current styling (styles.startWorkoutButton, styles.startWorkoutButtonText)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Move active workout banner to fixed bottom position (Mobile App)
userStories.description: As a React Native mobile user with an active workout, I want the active workout controls pinned at the bottom for consistent access.
acceptanceCriteria: When an active workout exists, the active workout banner replaces the Start Workout button at the bottom of HomeScreen.tsx
Banner shows workout type, elapsed time, and Strike/End Workout buttons
Banner is positioned outside the ScrollView at the bottom of the screen container
The original inline active workout banner (around lines 345-356) is removed from inside the ScrollView
ScrollView content is not obscured by the banner
Strike and End Workout button handlers still function correctly from the new position
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove burn buddy/squad badge from list cards (Web)
userStories.description: As a web user, I want a cleaner buddy/squad card design without the redundant badge label.
acceptanceCriteria: The <span> badge showing '🔥 Burn Buddy' or '🔥 Burn Squad' is removed from the combined buddy/squad list cards in apps/web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx (currently around line 482)
The rest of the card layout (name, avatar, streak count, last workout info) remains unchanged
No other fire emoji instances are affected (nav bar branding, streak counts)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove burn buddy/squad badge from list cards (Mobile App)
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want a cleaner buddy/squad card design without the redundant badge labels.
acceptanceCriteria: The '🔥 Burn Buddy' type label text is removed from buddy list cards in apps/mobile/src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx (currently around line 460)
The '🔥 Burn Squad' type label text is removed from squad list cards in HomeScreen.tsx (currently around line 486)
The rest of the card layout (name, avatar, streak count) remains unchanged
No other fire emoji instances are affected (header branding, streak counts)
Typecheck passes
